headline: How CVS’s Insulin Pricing Settlement and Dividend Move (CVS) Has Changed Its Investment Story
summary: Earlier in March 2026, CVS Health said it had reached a proposed settlement with the Federal Trade Commission over insulin pricing practices at its Caremark pharmacy benefit manager unit, while also declaring a quarterly dividend of US$0.665 per share payable on May 4, 2026, to shareholders of record on April 23, 2026. The proposed FTC settlement over insulin pricing puts CVS Health’s pharmacy benefit management model and its role in drug cost transparency under sharper regulatory and...

content.summary: Earlier in March 2026, CVS Health said it had reached a proposed settlement with the Federal Trade Commission over insulin pricing practices at its Caremark pharmacy benefit manager unit, while also declaring a quarterly dividend of US$0.665 per share payable on May 4, 2026, to shareholders of record on April 23, 2026. The proposed FTC settlement over insulin pricing puts CVS Health’s pharmacy benefit management model and its role in drug cost transparency under sharper regulatory and...

headline: Judge dismisses lawsuit by Musk's X Corp accusing advertisers of illegal boycott
summary: A U.S. judge on Thursday dismissed X Corp's antitrust lawsuit that accused the World Federation ‌of Advertisers and major companies including Mars, CVS Health ‌and Colgate-Palmolive of illegally boycotting billionaire entrepreneur Elon Musk’s social media company.  U.S. District ​Judge Jane Boyle in the federal court in Dallas said X failed to show it had suffered any harm under federal antitrust laws.  X Corp's lawsuit, filed in 2024, said the advertisers, acting through a ‌World Federation of Advertisers ⁠initiative called Global Alliance for Responsible Media, collectively withheld “billions of dollars in advertising revenue” from X, previously ⁠known as Twitter.

headline: Mark Cuban Calls $30,000 Corporate Healthcare Burden 'Insane,' Backs Bill To Break Up Big Medicine Giants UNH, CVS, CI
summary: Billionaire entrepreneur Mark Cuban is sounding the alarm on skyrocketing corporate healthcare costs, declaring them a primary driver of layoffs and throwing his full weight behind bipartisan legislation to dismantle healthcare conglomerates like UnitedHealth Group Inc., CVS Health Corp., and Cigna Group. headline: Court Dismisses X Lawsuit Alleging Brands Illegally Boycotted the Platform
summary: The suit accused companies including CVS Health and Lego of conspiring to withhold ad spending over X’s content policies.

content.title: Court Dismisses X Lawsuit Alleging Brands Illegally Boycotted the Platform
content.summary: The suit accused companies including CVS Health and Lego of conspiring to withhold ad spending over X’s content policies.
